value.reason: The cited page is an interactive visualization of Claude Code's context window, and it explicitly documents context compaction behavior. Specifically, the 'Skill descriptions' event entry states: "Unlike the rest of the startup content, this listing is not re-injected after `/compact`. Only skills you actually invoked get preserved." This directly confirms the `/compact` command exists, that it compresses/summarizes the conversation to free context window space, and that certain startup content (skill descriptions) is selectively re-injected or dropped based on usage — exactly what the cell description claims. The label `/compact` is the real command name used in the page. The description accurately reflects the page's content: manual compaction command, freeing context window space to enable longer sessions within the 200K token limit, with selective re-injection behavior for skill descriptions. No adjacent capabilities (MCP, persistent memory, prompt caching) are confused here.
